title: If Statements
description: Execute code only when a condition is true.

slides.title: Making Decisions 🧠
slides.content:
Programs need to make decisions based on different situations.

In C++, we use the `if` statement to specify a block of code to be executed *only* if a certain condition is `true`.

slides.title: The if Statement
slides.content: Notice that `if` is in lowercase letters.

quizData.question: When does the code inside an `if` block get executed?

options.text: Only when its condition evaluates to true
Only when its condition evaluates to false

quizData.explanation: An if block is executed only if its given condition is true.

slides.title: Variables in If 📦
slides.content: You can use variables to test conditions, too!

slides.title: Example
slides.content: Testing a variable against a number:

quizData.question: The condition of an `if` statement MUST be wrapped in parentheses ().
quizData.explanation: Yes, standard C++ syntax requires parentheses around the condition: if (condition) { ... }
